title: excoecaria baccata
intro: Excoecaria baccata, also known as the poison tree or millingtonia, is a tropical tree native to the coastal regions of the Indo-Pacific area, characterized by its toxic latex that contains a chemical called phorbol ester. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria baccata include treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds, as well as reducing fever and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and latex of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
